Notice Of Motion Andm Otion To Enforce Settlement Agreement Pursuant To Ccp Sec 664.6
Off Calendar May-30-2014 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Scottt A. Freedman, Esq. of Zacks & Freedman PC (415-56-8100) In Pro Per attorney for plaintiff/moving party
No appearance by defendant/opposing party

No opposition; Court Adopts the Tentative ruling as follows:
PLAINTIFF SCOTT FRIEDMAN's Motion To Enforce Settlement Agreement Pursuant To CCP Sec 664.6 is GRANTED. Defendants breached the terms of the stipulation. The judgment shall enter immediately and shall include the 10% interest on $52,500 (Mach 19, 2014 to April 30, 2014) and increased rental payment for the same period of time. Judgment shall be stayed for two weeks to provide Defendants an opportunity to comply with the terms of the settlement agreement. Maintenance issues are outside the scope of the settlement agreement and are not properly before the Court on this motion. Judge: Ronald E. Quidachay; Clerk: Jose Rios Merida; Not Reported. (D501)

MOTION FOR LEAVE TO Fiule First Amended X-Complt; Stip
Off Calendar Aug-06-2013 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: No appearance on this matter. RULING: The Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANTs MICHAEL SPOHN & CAROL SPOHN's MOTION FOR LEAVE TO file First Amended cross complaint is granted. There is a liberal policy in favor of amendment and no substantial evidence that the motion was brought in bad faith. Silver Organizations Ltd. v. Frank, (1990) 217 Cal.App.3d 94, 98-99. The trial date is continued to December 9, 2013. Order signed in open court. Judge: Marla J. Miller, Clerk: Lesley Fiscella, Reporter: Melanie Gheno CSR7489.
